 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	import { createHash } from 'crypto';
     2	
     3	/**
     4	 * Canonical MD-body normalization for cross-backend hashing (§5.2):
     5	 * LF line endings + exactly one trailing newline + Unicode NFC.
     6	 * Local-file storage (may carry CRLF / trailing blank lines) and Postgres
     7	 * jsonb storage (LF only) must produce byte-identical output here.
     8	 */
     9	export function canonicalizeMd(md: string): string {
    10	  const lf = md.replace(/\r\n?/g, '\n');
    11	  const trimmed = lf.replace(/\n+$/, '');
    12	  return `${trimmed.normalize('NFC')}\n`;
    13	}
    14	
    15	/** SHA-256 hex of the canonicalized MD body (§5.2). NOT over human fields. */
    16	export function mdHash(md: string): string {
    17	  return createHash('sha256').update(canonicalizeMd(md), 'utf8').digest('hex');
    18	}

     1	import type { Video } from '@/types';
     2	import type { ClassASignals, HumanSnapshot, HumanField, FieldState } from './types';
     3	import { mdHash } from './content-hash';
     4	
     5	// mdBody is the MD BODY the caller read from the blob store (BlobStore.get(p, video.summaryMd)).
     6	// NEVER hash video.summaryMd — it is a blob key/filename, not content (§5.2, Blocking ①).
     7	export function deriveClassASignals(video: Video, mdBody: string | null): ClassASignals {
     8	  const hasReal = video.mdGeneratedAt != null;
     9	  return {
    10	    summaryMdKey: video.summaryMd ?? null,
    11	    mdHash: mdBody != null ? mdHash(mdBody) : null,
    12	    docVersionMajor: video.docVersion?.major ?? 1,
    13	    mdGeneratedAt: video.mdGeneratedAt ?? video.processedAt ?? null,
    14	    mdCorrectionsHash: video.mdCorrectionsHash ?? null,
    15	    backfilled: !hasReal,
    16	  };
    17	}
    18	
    19	const FIELDS: HumanField[] = ['personalNote', 'personalScore', 'corrections'];
    20	
    21	export function deriveHumanSnapshot(video: Video): HumanSnapshot {
    22	  const provisional = video.updatedAt ?? video.processedAt;
    23	  const out = {} as HumanSnapshot;
    24	  for (const f of FIELDS) {
    25	    const value = video[f] as string | number | undefined;
    26	    const real = video.annotationsEditedAt?.[f];
    27	    const state: FieldState<string | number> = value === undefined && real === undefined
    28	      ? { value: undefined, editedAt: undefined, backfilled: false }
    29	      : { value, editedAt: real ?? provisional, backfilled: real === undefined };
    30	    out[f] = state;
    31	  }
    32	  return out;
    33	}

/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba lib/cloud-sync/reconcile-class-b.ts | sed -n '1,160p'" in /Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	import type { FieldState, HumanField, HumanSnapshot, VideoBaseline } from './types';
     2	
     3	export interface FieldMerge {
     4	  winner: 'local' | 'cloud' | 'equal';
     5	  value: string | number | undefined;
     6	  editedAt: string | undefined;
     7	  conflict: boolean;
     8	}
     9	
    10	type Baseline = { value?: string | number; editedAt?: string };
    11	
    12	/** Changed vs baseline is over the (value, editedAt) PAIR, not value alone (§5.4). */
    13	function changed(side: FieldState, base: Baseline): boolean {
    14	  return side.value !== base.value || side.editedAt !== base.editedAt;
    15	}
    16	
    17	function newer(a: string | undefined, b: string | undefined): boolean {
    18	  // returns true when a is strictly newer than b; undefined sorts oldest
    19	  return (a ?? '') > (b ?? '');
    20	}
    21	
    22	export function reconcileField(local: FieldState, cloud: FieldState, baseline: Baseline): FieldMerge {
    23	  // Equal VALUES never conflict (§5.4 row 1). But if their per-field timestamps differ, CONVERGE:
    24	  // return the newer-timestamp side as a NON-conflicting winner so the older side's editedAt is
    25	  // written forward and both replicas end identical — returning 'equal' here would skip the write
    26	  // and leave baseline/live timestamp drift (round-2 H1). Truly-equal pair → 'equal' (no write).
    27	  if (local.value === cloud.value) {
    28	    if (local.editedAt === cloud.editedAt) {
    29	      return { winner: 'equal', value: local.value, editedAt: local.editedAt, conflict: false };
    30	    }
    31	    return newer(local.editedAt, cloud.editedAt)
    32	      ? { winner: 'local', value: local.value, editedAt: local.editedAt, conflict: false }
    33	      : { winner: 'cloud', value: cloud.value, editedAt: cloud.editedAt, conflict: false };
    34	  }
    35	  const lChanged = changed(local, baseline);
    36	  const cChanged = changed(cloud, baseline);
    37	
    38	  if (lChanged && !cChanged) return { winner: 'local', value: local.value, editedAt: local.editedAt, conflict: false };
    39	  if (cChanged && !lChanged) return { winner: 'cloud', value: cloud.value, editedAt: cloud.editedAt, conflict: false };
    40	
    41	  // both changed (or neither vs an absent baseline but values differ) → newer per-field ts wins.
    42	  // A backfilled timestamp must never drive a destructive overwrite (§5.5) → conflict skip.
    43	  if (local.backfilled || cloud.backfilled) {
    44	    return { winner: 'equal', value: local.value, editedAt: local.editedAt, conflict: true };
    45	  }
    46	  const localWins = newer(local.editedAt, cloud.editedAt);
    47	  return localWins
    48	    ? { winner: 'local', value: local.value, editedAt: local.editedAt, conflict: true }
    49	    : { winner: 'cloud', value: cloud.value, editedAt: cloud.editedAt, conflict: true };
    50	}
    51	
    52	const FIELDS: HumanField[] = ['personalNote', 'personalScore', 'corrections'];
    53	
    54	export function reconcileHuman(
    55	  local: HumanSnapshot,
    56	  cloud: HumanSnapshot,
    57	  baseline: VideoBaseline['classB'],
    58	): Record<HumanField, FieldMerge> {
    59	  const out = {} as Record<HumanField, FieldMerge>;
    60	  for (const f of FIELDS) out[f] = reconcileField(local[f], cloud[f], baseline[f] ?? {});
    61	  return out;
    62	}

codex
A concrete issue has emerged in `readManifest`: it still collapses missing, corrupt, and unreadable into the empty baseline. That is not just telemetry loss; for one-sided videos it changes delete inference into additive resurrection. I’m validating this against the spec/review trail before deciding severity.
exec
/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-stage3-cloud-sync-design.md | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Stage 3 — Cloud Sync (local ↔ cloud reconciliation) — Design Spec
     2	
     3	**Status:** **v10 — CONVERGED** (dual adversarial review, 2026-07-17). Final round: **opus CONVERGED (0 B/H/M)**
     4	+ **Codex 0 Blocking/0 High** (2 Medium dispositioned: the §12.3 recompute contradiction FIXED; the
     5	carried-scalar-staleness Medium dispositioned as accepted **R9** per opus's deeper trace showing carry-verbatim
     6	is MD-consistent/safe). v10 also adds the `archived_not_synced` disclosure (R10) and softens over-absolute
     7	wording. The **two-class model** (generated=format/corrections-currency; human=per-field newer-wins) is
     8	reviewer-confirmed sound across v7→v10: v7 (Blockings) → v8 (1B+1H) → v9 (both 0 B/H) → **v10 CONVERGED**.
     9	Required code preconditions ship with M2a (§5.7). **Awaiting user approval → implementation plan (Phase 2).**
    10	Reviews: `.superpowers/sdd/cloud-sync-spec-*.md`.
    11	
    12	**Roadmap:** M2 of `docs/roadmap-to-launch.md`.
    13	
    14	**Goal:** Give the single author a **Cloud Sync** that reconciles their local research corpus with their own
    15	multi-tenant cloud account — so the cloud portal mirrors local work for peer sharing and multi-device
    16	access, a second device can hydrate from the cloud, and reconciliation matches how the two kinds of content
    17	actually behave.
    18	
    19	---
    20	
    21	## 1. Scope
    22	
    23	**M2 decomposition:**
    24	- **M2a — THIS spec:** local↔cloud reconciliation of each video's **summary** (LLM-generated MD + model) and
    25	  **human edits** (rating/note/corrections), both directions, per the two-class rules (§5). Honest
    26	  **additive** create with **baseline-aware** delete-suppression. Manual trigger.
    27	- **M2b — later slice (own spec):** deep-dive/dig **+ slide images** via the cloud-tokens → local-capture →
    28	  sync-back pipeline (§13), cross-replica tombstone deletes, background/auto-sync, true-conflict
    29	  loser-preservation.
    30	
    31	**In scope (M2a):** summary MD + model-JSON companion; human fields (`corrections`, `personalNote`,
    32	`personalScore`); playlist/video identity; union hydration; the two-class change signals + non-destructive
    33	backfill; Supabase-Auth login; per-playlist manifest; manual **Cloud Sync**.
    34	
    35	**Out of scope (M2a):** deep-dive/dig + slide images (M2b, §13); tombstone delete propagation; background
    36	sync; true-conflict loser-preservation; HTML/PDF transfer (regenerable cache).
    37	
    38	---
    39	
    40	## 2. Terminology
    41	
    42	| Term | Meaning |
    43	|---|---|
    44	| **Cloud Sync** | This feature: reconcile local corpus ↔ cloud tenant. (NOT the local **"Sync"** button = refresh a playlist's videos from YouTube.) |
    45	| **Replica** | One copy of the corpus: a local install, or the cloud tenant. |
    46	| **Class A — generated** | LLM output: **summary MD** + **model JSON**. Non-deterministic; reconciled by **format**, not recency (§5.3). |
    47	| **Class B — human** | Author edits: **`corrections`, `personalNote`, `personalScore`**. Reconciled **per field, newer-wins, additive**; preserved across every format (§5.4). |
    48	| **`docVersion`** | Format/style version stamped *at generation* (`CURRENT_DOC_VERSION`, `lib/doc-version.ts`). `.major` = MD content-format; `.minor` = HTML render style. **A format signal, not a timestamp.** |
    49	| **Companion** | The model-JSON blob, copied *with* the winning MD but never independently compared (§4.2). |
    50	
    51	---
    52	
    53	## 3. Identity (cross-replica keys) — *reviewed sound (rounds 1–6)*
    54	
    55	- **Playlist** = YouTube list-id = cloud `playlists.playlist_key` (`0001:13`, unique per `owner_id` `:17`);
    56	  `Principal.indexKey` abstracts local-path ↔ playlist_key (`lib/storage/principal.ts:7`).
    57	- **Video** = YouTube `video_id` (`types/index.ts:30`).
    58	- **Owner** = `auth.uid()`; composite FK `videos(playlist_id,owner_id)→playlists(id,owner_id)` (`0001:31-32`)
    59	  + forced RLS isolate tenants. No synthetic mapping. Peers get access via share tokens, not sync.
    60	
    61	---
    62	
    63	## 4. What syncs (M2a) — three classes
    64	
    65	### 4.1 Per-video field classification
    66	- **Class A — generated (reconcile by corrections-currency + format, §5.3):** summary **MD** (section-timestamp
    67	  `▶` markers ride inline in it); **model JSON** (`ModelEnvelope`), carried as a **companion** (§4.2), never a
    68	  compared source.
    69	- **Class A-companion scalars — CARRIED with the winning MD (NOT re-derived — round-v8 B-1):** `ratings`
    70	  (the 5 per-dimension values), `overallScore`, `videoType`, `audience`, `tags`, `tldr`, `takeaways`. These
    71	  live on the video record and power the list UI's sort/filter/cards. They are **copied verbatim from the
    72	  sender's record alongside the winning MD** — NOT recomputed on the receiver: `reconstructVideo` cannot
    73	  recover them (the MD frontmatter stores only the *average* `score`, so the 5 real `ratings` would be
    74	  fabricated as flat copies; `tldr`/`takeaways`/`tags` live in the MD-body quick-ref callout it never parses;
    75	  the `MagazineModel` companion carries only `{sections}`). The sender already holds correct values for its
    76	  MD, so carrying them is a pure, uncharged copy that stays consistent under normal operation (a mid-write
    77	  crash on the *sender* — MD-then-index in two steps — can leave the sender's own record transiently
    78	  inconsistent; a pre-existing local durability gap, not sync-introduced, self-healed on its next
    79	  regenerate/backfill; and an orphan-recovered record carries lower-fidelity but MD-derived scalars,
    80	  restored to full by a regen — residual R9). They are part of the atomic Class-A
    81	  record write (§7 step 4), never synced independently of their MD.
    82	- **Class B — independent human annotations (per-field 3-way merge, §5.4):** `personalNote`, `personalScore`.
    83	- **`corrections` — human field AND the MD's generation input (special):** reconciled as a human field (§5.4,
    84	  newer-wins, preserved) AND tracked as the MD's **corrections-currency** — because `corrections` is *applied
    85	  into* the MD via `fixSummary`/regenerate (`app/api/videos/[id]/regenerate/route.ts:51-71`), the MD records
    86	  `mdCorrectionsHash` and §5.3 never lets a stale MD overwrite a corrected one.
    87	- **Replica-local / fetched (NOT synced, but still locally writable):** `title` (YouTube-fetched, no
    88	  author-edit path), `position`, `serialNumber`, `playlistIndex`, `removedFromPlaylist`, `updatedAt`,
    89	  `summaryReady`. **`archived` is replica-local in M2a but stays fully writable** — it has *two* semantics
    90	  (membership-archive via `reconcile_membership` `0007:60-71`, and a *manual* Archive toggle written via
    91	  `update_video_annotations`, `app/api/videos/[id]/archive/route.ts`); because the two are entangled on one
    92	  field, M2a does **not** sync `archived` (avoids the membership flip-flop) but must **keep it in the
    93	  annotation-writer allowlist** so the manual Archive button keeps working (round-v8 H-1). Cleanly
    94	  separating manual-archive (a syncable human preference) from membership-archive is **M2b**.
    95	- **Regenerable cache (never synced):** HTML, PDF (deterministic re-render from MD + model).
    96	
    97	### 4.2 Model JSON companion — sync-transfer only, serve path UNCHANGED (rounds 2–6)
    98	Model JSON is a **non-deterministic, `GENERATOR_VERSION`-axed, charged, self-healing cache** (Gemini
    99	transform; lazily regenerated on serve — `lib/html-doc/model-store.ts`, `read-model.ts`). It is **never**
   100	hash-compared. Its freshness is handled **only at sync-transfer**, leaving the serve path (`isFresh`,
   101	`readTitleStableModel`, the share route, the over-budget fallback) **unchanged** — because a global gate
   102	change would re-charge the whole corpus and dark-serve every share (round-4 BLK-1).
   103	- `ModelEnvelope` gains an OPTIONAL **`sourceMdHash`** — an **MD-body-only** digest (§5.2), set going
   104	  forward; the schema is **forward-tolerant** (old readers ignore the new key). Legacy envelopes lack it.
   105	- On a Class-A MD-transfer: ship the sender's model as a companion **iff** `sourceMdHash == mdHash(winning
   106	  MD)`; else **delete the receiver's model blob** (→ lazy regen on the **owner's** next serve). A **shared
   107	  (anonymous)** view of that specific video is not-ready until the owner serves (the share route is
   108	  generation-free — residual **R7**); sync reports these as `share_needs_owner_serve` (§7 step 6).
   109	
   110	---
   111	
   112	## 5. Reconcile model — two independent per-video reconciles
   113	
   114	Each video reconciles its **Class A** and **Class B** state **independently**: a format upgrade to the MD
   115	never touches the human fields, and a human-field edit never touches the MD. This is the core v7 change.
   116	
   117	### 5.1 Signals (per class)
   118	- **Class A:** `docVersion.major` (format — the decider), `mdHash` (the MD-body-only §5.2 digest = the
   119	  envelope's `sourceMdHash`), `mdGeneratedAt` (UTC, a **tie-break only**, never a quality signal), and
   120	  **`mdCorrectionsHash`** — the §5.2 hash of the `corrections` value this MD was generated/fixed from, for
   121	  corrections-currency (§5.3). `docVersion.minor` (HTML style) is **ignored** — sync moves MD, not HTML;
   122	  each app re-renders the MD in its own current style, so nothing is "stuck" in an old style.
   123	- **Class B / `corrections`:** each field's value + a **PER-FIELD** timestamp,
   124	  `annotationsEditedAt.{personalNote, personalScore, corrections}` — a same-field tie compares *that field's*
   125	  real edit time (round-v7 Blocking B1: a single per-video timestamp is contaminated by unrelated field edits
   126	  and would pick the older edit).
   127	- **Stamping:** `mdGeneratedAt` + `mdCorrectionsHash` on MD generation (`persist_summary` `0009`; local
   128	  `pipeline.ts`). Each human-field write stamps **only that field's** `annotationsEditedAt`
   129	  (`update_video_annotations` `0016`; `merge_video_data`/`updateVideoFields` for `corrections` — **conditional
   130	  on a Class-B key being present**, so a generic MD-finalize / artifact / membership write never bumps a human
   131	  timestamp, round-v7 L-1; the local index writer). Membership writers → no restamp.
   132	- **Sync-path writes carry the SOURCE timestamp, NOT `now()` (round-v7 H1):** when sync applies a winning
   133	  human value to the receiver, it sets that field's `annotationsEditedAt` to the **source's** value — the
   134	  writers accept an explicit timestamp on the sync path (distinct from the user-edit path, which stamps
   135	  `now()`) — so the baseline records true authorship and later ties compare real edit times.
   136	
   137	### 5.2 Canonical `mdHash` (rounds 1–3, 5)
   138	`mdHash` is an **MD-body-only** canonical digest — a shared impl (`lib/cloud-sync/content-hash.ts`) called
   139	by both replicas: MD bytes normalized to LF + fixed trailing-newline + NFC, SHA-256 hex. It is **not** over
   140	the human fields (they reconcile separately, §5.4), so a `personalNote` edit never invalidates the model
   141	(round-5 M-1). §10 requires cross-backend golden fixtures (local file vs Postgres `jsonb` → equal).
   142	
   143	### 5.3 Class A reconcile (generated MD + model) — corrections-currency FIRST, then format
   144	Recency does **not** decide generated content (the LLM is non-deterministic; a newer generation is not
   145	"better"). But `corrections` is *applied into* the MD, so a **corrected** MD is not an equivalent variant of
   146	an uncorrected one (round-v7 Codex-H1). `corrections` is reconciled **first** (§5.4); an MD is
   147	**corrections-current** iff `mdCorrectionsHash == hash(reconciled corrections)`. Priority: **corrections-
   148	current > format > recency-tiebreak.**
   149	
   150	Evaluate **corrections-currency first**, so the `mdHash`-equal skip never hides a stale summary (round-v8 H-1):
   151	
   152	| Situation | Action |
   153	|---|---|
   154	| `mdHash` equal **and** (both corrections-current, or both equally stale-and-format-equal) | **skip** — but if **both are stale** vs the reconciled corrections, still **flag `needs_regen`** (identical stale MDs must not bypass the R8 report) |
   155	| One MD corrections-current, the other corrections-stale | **corrections-current wins** — never overwrite a corrected MD with a stale higher-format one. Copy it (+ companion §4.2 + carried scalars). |
   156	| Both corrections-current (or both equally stale), `docVersion.major` differs | **higher `major` wins** (format upgrade; never downgrade) |
   157	| Both corrections-current, same `major`, `mdHash` differs (equivalent LLM variants) | **unify** — newer `mdGeneratedAt` wins; copy so the prose **converges** (intention-respecting tie-break, not a quality claim; avoids undoing a deliberate re-generation) |
   158	| **Neither** MD reflects the reconciled corrections (both stale) | keep the higher-major MD but **flag `needs_regen`** (report it, §7 step 6) — the author regenerates to apply the corrections at the current format; sync **never fabricates** a corrected MD (residual **R8**) |
   159	| Present on only one side (never in this replica's baseline) | **copy** (hydrate / publish) |
   160	
   161	**Every Class-A transfer carries the sender's companion scalars** (§4.1: `ratings`, `overallScore`,
   162	`videoType`, `audience`, `tags`, `tldr`, `takeaways`) **verbatim with the winning MD** — a pure, uncharged
   163	copy of values the sender already holds for that MD (NOT re-derived on the receiver — round-v8 B-1), written
   164	in the same atomic Class-A record (§7 step 4) so cards/sort/filter never drift. No data-loss: a losing MD is
   165	corrections-stale-or-equivalent, and the `corrections` instruction survives (§5.4) and re-applies on regen.
   166	Clock skew stays non-load-bearing (a same-format tie just picks one equivalent variant).
   167	
   168	### 5.4 Class B / `corrections` reconcile — per-field 3-way merge, clear-aware (runs BEFORE §5.3)
   169	Human fields (`personalNote`, `personalScore`, `corrections`) are precious and **carried across every
   170	format**. Each reconciles **independently** against the manifest baseline (§8). **Absence is a value** (a
   171	*clear*), not "never had" (round-v7 H-2), **and "changed vs baseline" is judged on the field's
   172	`(value, annotationsEditedAt)` pair, not the value alone** (round-v8 M-1): a **clear stamps**
   173	`annotationsEditedAt` (the timestamp outlives the removed value, in both the live record and the manifest
   174	baseline), and a **same-value re-add** (clear then re-type the same text) counts as *changed* because its
   175	timestamp advanced — so it is tie-broken by newer-wins, not silently dropped. The table below reads "changed"
   176	as "the `(value, annotationsEditedAt)` pair differs from baseline":
   177	
   178	| Per-field state vs baseline | Action |
   179	|---|---|
   180	| L == C | no action |
   181	| Only one side changed vs baseline (incl. a **clear** = baseline-present→absent) | take the changed side — **propagate the edit or the clear** |
   182	| **Both** changed vs baseline (different values, incl. one cleared) | newer **per-field `annotationsEditedAt`** wins + log (R1) |
   183	| No baseline (fresh device) + differ | newer per-field `annotationsEditedAt` wins + log |
   184	| Present one side, absent other, **no baseline** | copy (additive hydration) |
   185	
   186	Independent fields merge cleanly — a note edit on one side + a score edit on the other **both survive**; a
   187	cleared field is **not** resurrected (with a baseline, present-vs-absent is a real change → the clear
   188	propagates). A human field is **never** lost to a Class-A format change (they reconcile independently).
   189	`corrections` reconciles here too and, because it feeds §5.3's corrections-currency, is reconciled **first**.
   190	
   191	### 5.5 Backfill (legacy records) — non-destructive (round-2 H-C)
   192	Legacy records lack `mdGeneratedAt`/`mdCorrectionsHash`/`annotationsEditedAt`. A one-time backfill records
   193	**provisional** values (MD: `processedAt`; human: `updated_at`) flagged as backfilled, and a backfilled
   194	timestamp **never drives a destructive overwrite**: a same-format Class-A tie with a backfilled
   195	`mdGeneratedAt` just picks one equivalent variant (harmless); a Class-B same-field conflict with a backfilled
   196	per-field `annotationsEditedAt` resolves to **conflict → skip + log**, never overwrite. Format (Class A) and 3-way field merge (Class B)
   197	carry the real decisions, so backfill is far less load-bearing than in the single-class model.
   198	
   199	### 5.6 Presence & deletes — additive + baseline-aware (rounds 2–4)
   200	- One-sided, never in this replica's baseline → additive **create** (a pure metadata/doc copy that **never**
   201	  routes through the metered enqueue `lib/job-queue/producer.ts`, never consumes `spend_ledger`, never
   202	  resurrects derived cache).
   203	- In this replica's baseline but **absent on the other side** → **remote delete**: do not re-create.
   204	- In this replica's baseline but **absent on this side** (this replica deleted it) → do not re-create
   205	  locally, do not delete on the other (no propagation — M2b tombstones).
   206	- **Residual R2:** a replica with **no baseline** (fresh device / lost manifest) can't tell "deleted
   207	  elsewhere" from "never seen" → may re-create (resurrect). Full delete-safety = M2b tombstones. No local
   208	  delete-intent marker (round-2 H-A showed it has no sound lifecycle).
   209	
   210	### 5.7 Required schema / migration changes (NOT optional — the reconcile depends on them)
   211	The reconcile signals are new; these ship **with** M2a (round-v7 H-4/M-1/M-2 flagged that the code
   212	preconditions aren't yet true):
   213	- **`VideoSchema` +** `mdGeneratedAt?`, `mdCorrectionsHash?`, and per-field `annotationsEditedAt?: {personalNote?,
   214	  personalScore?, corrections?}` (datetimes). One-time backfill per §5.5.
   215	- **`ModelEnvelopeSchema`:** add `sourceMdHash?: string` **and drop `.strict()`** (→ ignore unknown keys) so a
   216	  new-writer envelope doesn't make an old reader's `readModelEnvelope` return null → notReady/re-charge
   217	  (round-5 M-2, still `.strict()` in `model-store.ts:22`).
   218	- **`update_video_annotations` (`0016`) allowlist → `{personalScore, personalNote, corrections, archived}`** —
   219	  **ADD** `corrections` (Class B, currently dropped); **KEEP** `archived` so the manual Archive button keeps
   220	  working (round-v8 H-1 — `archived` is replica-local for *sync* but still user-writable). Each write restamps
   221	  **only the changed Class-B field's** `annotationsEditedAt` (a `corrections`/`personalNote`/`personalScore`
   222	  write — including a **clear**, which stamps the timestamp while removing the value, round-v8 M-1); an
   223	  `archived`-only write restamps nothing (not synced).
   224	- **`merge_video_data` restamp is CONDITIONAL** on a Class-B key in the patch (it is a blind generic merge also
   225	  used for MD-finalize / artifact / membership writes — round-v7 L-1).
   226	- **Writers accept an explicit timestamp on the sync path** (vs `now()` on the user-edit path — round-v7 H1).
   227	- **`persist_summary` / local `pipeline.ts`** stamp `mdGeneratedAt` + `mdCorrectionsHash` on generation, and
   228	  the Class-A record write must carry the **companion scalars** (§4.1) so a synced MD lands with its own
   229	  correct `ratings`/`tldr`/… (round-v8 B-1).
   230	
   231	---
   232	
   233	## 6. Auth (local → cloud) — *reviewed sound; hardened storage*
   234	
   235	Local uses the **same Supabase Auth login** as the web app; all cloud I/O is under that user session →
   236	RLS-scoped to `auth.uid()`. **No service-role key on the local machine**; a server-mediated sync endpoint
   237	derives `owner_id` from the session, resolves playlists by `(auth.uid(), playlist_key)`, never from a
   238	client-supplied owner id. Refresh token → **OS keychain** preferred; file fallback mode 600 + parent-dir
   239	check + gitignore + fail-closed on broad perms; theft = full same-tenant access (no cross-tenant break);
   240	sign-out clears it. No session → refuse with a `cloud-sync login` hint.
   241	
   242	---
   243	
   244	## 7. Sync run (flow)
   245	
   246	1. **Playlist set = UNION** of local-registry `playlist_key`s (§7.1) ∪ `SELECT playlist_key FROM playlists
   247	   WHERE owner_id = auth.uid()`. One-sided playlists created on the other (subject to §5.6). A fresh device
   248	   (empty local) thus pulls the full cloud corpus.
   249	2. **Per playlist**, enumerate the union of `video_id`s via `MetadataStore`.
   250	3. **Per video**, run the **Class A** reconcile (§5.3) and the **Class B** reconcile (§5.4) independently.
   251	4. **Class A MD transfer is per-video atomic**, aligned with the existing staged→committed→promoted protocol
   252	   (`consistency.ts`, `summary-handler.ts`): stage the winning MD under an idempotency key, verify, promote,
   253	   **then** finalize the receiver record in **one atomic update** with the **complete** MD-owned tuple —
   254	   `summaryMd`/artifact status, `mdHash`, `docVersion`, `mdGeneratedAt`, **`mdCorrectionsHash`**, and **all
   255	   carried companion scalars** (§4.1) — so currency and cards/sort/filter never lag the MD (round-v8 M-2/L-1).
   256	   Metadata never advertises the new `mdHash` until the MD is promoted; a crash leaves staged objects + an
   257	   unadvanced baseline; re-run heals. The **companion model** blob is best-effort, outside the MD's atomic
   258	   commit (a lost companion self-heals via §4.2). **Class B field writes** are small record updates applied
   259	   after the merge (§5.4).
   260	5. **Update the manifest (§8) strictly AFTER** the receiver commit is verified durable — verifying the

docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-stage3-cloud-sync-design.md:199:### 5.6 Presence & deletes — additive + baseline-aware (rounds 2–4)
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-stage3-cloud-sync-design.md:200:- One-sided, never in this replica's baseline → additive **create** (a pure metadata/doc copy that **never**
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-stage3-cloud-sync-design.md:203:- In this replica's baseline but **absent on the other side** → **remote delete**: do not re-create.
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-stage3-cloud-sync-design.md:204:- In this replica's baseline but **absent on this side** (this replica deleted it) → do not re-create
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-stage3-cloud-sync-design.md:205:  locally, do not delete on the other (no propagation — M2b tombstones).
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-stage3-cloud-sync-design.md:206:- **Residual R2:** a replica with **no baseline** (fresh device / lost manifest) can't tell "deleted
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-stage3-cloud-sync-design.md:207:  elsewhere" from "never seen" → may re-create (resurrect). Full delete-safety = M2b tombstones. No local
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-stage3-cloud-sync-design.md:208:  delete-intent marker (round-2 H-A showed it has no sound lifecycle).
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-stage3-cloud-sync-design.md:247:   WHERE owner_id = auth.uid()`. One-sided playlists created on the other (subject to §5.6). A fresh device
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-stage3-cloud-sync-design.md:257:   unadvanced baseline; re-run heals. The **companion model** blob is best-effort, outside the MD's atomic
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-stage3-cloud-sync-design.md:260:5. **Update the manifest (§8) strictly AFTER** the receiver commit is verified durable — verifying the
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-stage3-cloud-sync-design.md:262:   human field `(value, annotationsEditedAt)` pairs. Never advance a baseline for a partial transfer.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:29:1. Does the guard cover EVERY consumer of a possibly-unreadable body, or only the two-sided reconcile path? Audit every other `readMdBody` / `blob.get` call in `lib/cloud-sync/*` — companion (`companion.ts`, `decideCompanion` / `sourceMdHash` comparison), `transferClassA`'s own reads, the manifest/backfill paths, `registry.ts`. Can a null-because-unreadable still be read as a semantic fact anywhere else (e.g. "the model was not generated from this MD" → deleting a companion model, or a Class-B/backfill decision)?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:30:2. Is throwing the right response versus skipping? A record that PERMANENTLY advertises a `summaryMd` whose blob is genuinely gone (real storage drift, not transient) now errors on EVERY run forever and never advances a baseline. Trace what that does to delete-inference, to `report.errors` growth, and to a user's ability to ever complete a sync run. Is there a case where this new throw makes a previously-working sync fail?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:31:3. Does the guard change behavior for the legitimate summary-less video (`summaryMd == null`) and the one-sided-hydration case (M-R2-2)? Both must still work. Confirm against the tests at `tests/integration/cloud-sync/e2e.int.test.ts`.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:32:4. Are the two new B1 regression tests honest — do they fail for the RIGHT reason when the guard is removed, and do they assert across two runs?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:35:The B1 root cause is a **semantic conflation across a module boundary**: a value that means "absent" in one module is produced by a failure in another. Hunt for siblings of that shape anywhere on the sync path — not just for blobs. Candidates worth tracing: a swallowed error that yields a default/empty value which a caller then treats as fact (empty index vs failed read; absent manifest vs unreadable manifest; a missing record vs a failed query; `readIndex` on a store error; `''` vs absent playlist metadata).
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:38:- Baseline-advance correctness on every branch; no advance without a durable write; every "seen" video gets one (delete-inference).
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:40:- Atomicity: durable-before-advertise, manifest-after-commit.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:1574:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-claude.md:94:### B1 (BLOCKING) — `mdHash == null` conflates "has no MD" with "MD is unreadable", so an unreadable blob silently destroys the other replica's body and launders it into an agreed baseline
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:1578:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-claude.md:106:deleted; seeds a cloud row advertising `summaryMd` + a `promoted` artifact with no readable body):
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:1596:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-claude.md:194:  new one-sided-hydration branches. The only laundering path found is B1.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:1607:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:29:1. Does the guard cover EVERY consumer of a possibly-unreadable body, or only the two-sided reconcile path? Audit every other `readMdBody` / `blob.get` call in `lib/cloud-sync/*` — companion (`companion.ts`, `decideCompanion` / `sourceMdHash` comparison), `transferClassA`'s own reads, the manifest/backfill paths, `registry.ts`. Can a null-because-unreadable still be read as a semantic fact anywhere else (e.g. "the model was not generated from this MD" → deleting a companion model, or a Class-B/backfill decision)?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:1608:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:30:2. Is throwing the right response versus skipping? A record that PERMANENTLY advertises a `summaryMd` whose blob is genuinely gone (real storage drift, not transient) now errors on EVERY run forever and never advances a baseline. Trace what that does to delete-inference, to `report.errors` growth, and to a user's ability to ever complete a sync run. Is there a case where this new throw makes a previously-working sync fail?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:1609:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:31:3. Does the guard change behavior for the legitimate summary-less video (`summaryMd == null`) and the one-sided-hydration case (M-R2-2)? Both must still work. Confirm against the tests at `tests/integration/cloud-sync/e2e.int.test.ts`.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:1610:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:32:4. Are the two new B1 regression tests honest — do they fail for the RIGHT reason when the guard is removed, and do they assert across two runs?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:1612:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:35:The B1 root cause is a **semantic conflation across a module boundary**: a value that means "absent" in one module is produced by a failure in another. Hunt for siblings of that shape anywhere on the sync path — not just for blobs. Candidates worth tracing: a swallowed error that yields a default/empty value which a caller then treats as fact (empty index vs failed read; absent manifest vs unreadable manifest; a missing record vs a failed query; `readIndex` on a store error; `''` vs absent playlist metadata).

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:1843:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:24:2. **H-R2-2** — `digDeeperMd: null` removed from `transferClassA`'s `completeTuple`. VERIFY: with `digDeeperMd` preserved but the MD BODY replaced by the winner's, is the retained dig doc now semantically stale in a way that misleads a consumer (`lib/html-doc/build-doc-html.ts:75,86`, `app/api/videos/[id]/dig-state/route.ts`, `lib/pdf/pdf-path.ts`)? Specifically: dig sections are anchored to summary section timestamps/anchors — if the winner MD has different sections, does merging the preserved dig produce wrong or orphaned anchors? Weigh that against the cost of destroying paid content. Is `digDeeperHtml: null` sufficient to force the re-merge? Is the additive path (`sanitizeAdditiveVideo`, which still nulls `digDeeperMd`) still correct given it targets a receiver with no existing row?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:1844:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:25:3. **M-R2-2** — the corrections guard narrowed to `correctionsUnresolved && la.mdHash != null && ca.mdHash != null`, with `deriveClassASignals` hoisted above the guard. VERIFY: the hoist claims to be behavior-neutral because derivation is pure — confirm `readMdBody` has no side effects and that moving TWO blob reads earlier cannot change ordering/error behavior (e.g. a blob read that throws now aborts the video BEFORE the Class-B baseline would have been written — is that a behavior change, and is it the right one?). Confirm the WB-B1 intent still holds exactly for the both-have-MD case. Confirm the one-sided hydration case cannot destroy anything or record a false agreement.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:1849:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:67:1. **WB-B1 (Blocking) — corrections no-write conflict drove a destructive Class-A copy.** Fix: in `runSync` (`lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts`), when `merges.corrections.winner === 'equal' && merges.corrections.conflict`, skip the Class-A copy entirely, count `needsRegen`, and write `buildCorrectionsUnresolvedBaseline` (carries the PREVIOUS classA baseline, or an honest `{docVersionMajor:0, mdGeneratedAt:null, mdCorrectionsHash:null, mdHash:null}` placeholder on first sync). VERIFY: is the guard placed BEFORE every write path (including the companion transfer and any archived/delete handling)? Does the `continue` skip anything that MUST still run (delete-inference "seen" marking, report counters, companion, archived sync)? Is `report.archivedNotSynced` incremented correctly and only there? Does the placeholder baseline (docVersionMajor 0) cause a wrong decision anywhere that DOES read the Class-A baseline — confirm reconcileClassA truly never reads it.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:2354:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:4774:  ● cloud-sync §10 end-to-end scenarios › WB-H1/H-R2-1: additive create with a promoted summaryMd but no blob throws before claiming a receiver slot; no row, no baseline (2 runs)
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:2368:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:4933:Evidence: [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:160) now throws before `ensureReceiverSlot` at line 164, so no `setPlaylistMeta`, staged blob write, `claimVideoSlot`, receiver row, or baseline can happen after an unreadable advertised MD body. The remaining `if (video.summaryMd && mdBody != null)` at line 167 is redundant after the guard, but harmless; it does not hide a path because the only alternative is a legitimate summary-less row. I temporarily moved the guard back and the H-R2-1 integration test failed on the bare local row assertion, then restored the file.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:2371:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:4939:Evidence: [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:491) derives signals before the narrowed guard, and [lib/cloud-sync/backfill.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/backfill.ts:7) is pure over the record plus supplied MD body. The hoist can surface blob-read errors earlier, but that is the right behavior: baseline must not advance if Class-A evidence is unreadable. The both-have-MD conflict path still takes the WB-B1 skip at [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:501). The one-sided hydration path writes the copied MD only after `transferClassA` lands, preserves unresolved corrections in Class-B baseline, and continues to report `needsRegen`.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:2435:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:19:1. **WB-B1 (Blocking) — corrections no-write conflict drove a destructive Class-A copy.** Fix: in `runSync` (`lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts`), when `merges.corrections.winner === 'equal' && merges.corrections.conflict`, skip the Class-A copy entirely, count `needsRegen`, and write `buildCorrectionsUnresolvedBaseline` (carries the PREVIOUS classA baseline, or an honest `{docVersionMajor:0, mdGeneratedAt:null, mdCorrectionsHash:null, mdHash:null}` placeholder on first sync). VERIFY: is the guard placed BEFORE every write path (including the companion transfer and any archived/delete handling)? Does the `continue` skip anything that MUST still run (delete-inference "seen" marking, report counters, companion, archived sync)? Is `report.archivedNotSynced` incremented correctly and only there? Does the placeholder baseline (docVersionMajor 0) cause a wrong decision anywhere that DOES read the Class-A baseline — confirm reconcileClassA truly never reads it.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:2784:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3114:lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:384: *  baseline) record an HONEST unresolved placeholder (mdHash null → "no agreed MD"); Class-A baseline
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:2785:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3121:WB-B1: GENUINELY FIXED. The guard is before Class-A signal derivation, transfer, companion transfer, normal archived counter, and normal baseline advance in [sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:471). It still logs Class-B conflicts before the guard, increments `needsRegen`, increments `archivedNotSynced` only for archived divergence, writes a baseline so delete inference has seen the video, then continues. `reconcileClassA` takes only live `ClassASignals`, not manifest Class-A baselines, so the `{ docVersionMajor: 0, mdHash: null }` placeholder is not read by the Class-A decision code.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:2788:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3132:Scenario: cloud-only video advertises `summaryMd`, but the MD blob is absent. Run 1 calls `ensureReceiverSlot` before the unreadable-body check, creating a bare local row, then throws at line 159 and does not write a baseline. Run 2 now sees both sides present, not an additive create. Since both MD body reads are null, `reconcileClassA` returns `skip`; `runSync` writes a manifest baseline at line 512 even though no receiver summary was ever durably copied and the source still advertises an unreadable summary. This masks the original corruption after one report and turns a failed additive copy into a false “seen/agreed no-MD” state.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:2789:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3134:Fix: validate `video.summaryMd`/`mdBody` before `ensureReceiverSlot`, or wrap `copyAdditiveVideo` in rollback that deletes the claimed receiver slot and any staged/final blob if any later step fails. The baseline should not be able to advance from a bare receiver slot created by a failed additive copy.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:2791:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3150:WB-B1: GENUINELY FIXED. The guard is before Class-A signal derivation, transfer, companion transfer, normal archived counter, and normal baseline advance in [sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:471). It still logs Class-B conflicts before the guard, increments `needsRegen`, increments `archivedNotSynced` only for archived divergence, writes a baseline so delete inference has seen the video, then continues. `reconcileClassA` takes only live `ClassASignals`, not manifest Class-A baselines, so the `{ docVersionMajor: 0, mdHash: null }` placeholder is not read by the Class-A decision code.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:2794:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3161:Scenario: cloud-only video advertises `summaryMd`, but the MD blob is absent. Run 1 calls `ensureReceiverSlot` before the unreadable-body check, creating a bare local row, then throws at line 159 and does not write a baseline. Run 2 now sees both sides present, not an additive create. Since both MD body reads are null, `reconcileClassA` returns `skip`; `runSync` writes a manifest baseline at line 512 even though no receiver summary was ever durably copied and the source still advertises an unreadable summary. This masks the original corruption after one report and turns a failed additive copy into a false “seen/agreed no-MD” state.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:2795:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3163:Fix: validate `video.summaryMd`/`mdBody` before `ensureReceiverSlot`, or wrap `copyAdditiveVideo` in rollback that deletes the claimed receiver slot and any staged/final blob if any later step fails. The baseline should not be able to advance from a bare receiver slot created by a failed additive copy.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:2798:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-codex.md:28:3. **ATOMICITY.** Manifest baseline written ONLY after the receiver tuple verifies durable. Blob durable BEFORE the record advertises `artifacts.summaryMd.status='promoted'`. Additive create verifies the receiver row exists (readIndex) before the baseline (cloud upsertVideo silently no-ops on an absent row → ensureReceiverSlot creates it). transferClassA's fix (put-overwrite the final key with verified staged bytes, then updateVideoFields) preserves durable-before-finalize. Crash before verify leaves the baseline unadvanced (re-run heals).

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:3636:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-codex.md:7474:   Scenario: source video has `summaryMd` and `artifacts.summaryMd.status='promoted'`, but the source blob read returns `null` due to storage drift/corruption/RLS miss. `copyAdditiveVideo` skips the blob write because `mdBody == null`, but `sanitizeAdditiveVideo` preserves the sender’s `artifacts.summaryMd`. docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:7573:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:4933:Evidence: [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:160) now throws before `ensureReceiverSlot` at line 164, so no `setPlaylistMeta`, staged blob write, `claimVideoSlot`, receiver row, or baseline can happen after an unreadable advertised MD body. The remaining `if (video.summaryMd && mdBody != null)` at line 167 is redundant after the guard, but harmless; it does not hide a path because the only alternative is a legitimate summary-less row. I temporarily moved the guard back and the H-R2-1 integration test failed on the bare local row assertion, then restored the file.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:7576:docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:4939:Evidence: [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:491) derives signals before the narrowed guard, and [lib/cloud-sync/backfill.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/backfill.ts:7) is pure over the record plus supplied MD body. The hoist can surface blob-read errors earlier, but that is the right behavior: baseline must not advance if Class-A evidence is unreadable. The one-sided hydration path writes the copied MD only after `transferClassA` lands, preserves unresolved corrections in Class-B baseline, and continues to report `needsRegen`.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:7919:Throwing is the right response for this fix. A permanently missing advertised blob will produce a per-video `report.errors` entry every run, but `runSync` catches per video and continues processing the rest. More importantly, it does not write a false baseline, does not infer agreement, and does not overwrite the other side. The user can repair by restoring the blob, clearing the stale `summaryMd`, or regenerating intentionally.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:7921:The two new B1 regression tests are honest. They seed a row that advertises promoted `summaryMd` while omitting the blob, assert byte preservation, assert no baseline, and repeat across two runs. Removing the guard would fail on the intended destructive-copy/laundering assertions.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:7940:  - baseline advance
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:7941:- The one-sided/additive path was already guarded at [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:160), before receiver slot creation.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:7945:- M-R2-2 still works: a real one-sided MD hydration with the other side `summaryMd == null` is not blocked.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:7947:Throwing is the right response for this fix. A permanently missing advertised blob will produce a per-video `report.errors` entry every run, but `runSync` catches per video and continues processing the rest. More importantly, it does not write a false baseline, does not infer agreement, and does not overwrite the other side. The user can repair by restoring the blob, clearing the stale `summaryMd`, or regenerating intentionally.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:7949:The two new B1 regression tests are honest. They seed a row that advertises promoted `summaryMd` while omitting the blob, assert byte preservation, assert no baseline, and repeat across two runs. Removing the guard would fail on the intended destructive-copy/laundering assertions.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:16:Trail (docs are the audit record): R1 `32a164c` (1 Blocking + 2 High) → R2 `1f54c60` (2 High, one a regression from R1's fix) → R3 `3bc8cc7` (**1 Blocking**, B1) → R4 `66fe6e5` (3 High: H1 companion-model delete, H2 a regression from B1's fix, H3 playlist-title wipe). Reviews: `docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync{,-v2,-v3,-v4}-rereview-{codex,claude}.md`.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:25:1. **H1** — `decideCompanion` now takes a tri-state `SenderModelRead`; `unknown` ⇒ `{kind:'noop'}` (leave the receiver's model, do NOT set `shareNeedsOwnerServe`). VERIFY: is `readSenderModel` (`sync-run.ts`) correct in mapping a null envelope to `none` only when `sender.blob.provesAbsence`? On the LOCAL sender a null can also mean **corrupt/schema-invalid** (readModelEnvelope parses + validates) — is deleting the receiver's model right in that case, or should corrupt be distinguished from absent too? Does `noop` leave any inconsistency (receiver keeps a model whose `sourceMdHash` no longer matches the winning MD — trace the serve path's drift guard and say whether that is safe)?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:27:3. **H3** — three layers: `LocalPlaylist.playlistTitle`, `playlistMetaFor` merging both registries, `ensureReceiverSlot` reading before writing to carry the receiver's own title forward. VERIFY each. **Specifically: I mutation-tested layer 3 (removed the `?? idx.playlistTitle` carry-forward) and NO test failed** — layers 1+2 already cover the tested scenario. Determine whether layer 3 is REACHABLE at all: is there a legitimate input where `playlistMetaFor` yields no title but the receiver row has one (e.g. a cloud playlist absent from `listPlaylists`, a playlist with zero videos, a local-only data root)? If reachable, that is a test-coverage finding; if unreachable, say so and recommend dropping it or documenting it as deliberate dead defense-in-depth. Also: `ensureReceiverSlot` now does `readIndex` BEFORE `setPlaylistMeta` and reuses that same snapshot for the row-exists check — confirm no TOCTOU or ordering regression.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:31:- Every remaining place the sync path treats emptiness/absence as a semantic fact: `readIndex` returning the empty-index sentinel (is an absent playlist distinguishable from a failed read on BOTH stores?), `readManifest` on a corrupt/unreadable manifest, `listPlaylists`, `claimVideoSlot`, `exists` callers, any `catch { return <default> }`, `?? null` / `?? ''` in a write payload (H3 was `playlist_title ?? null` — are there siblings in other upsert/update payloads, e.g. `merge_video_data`, `setPlaylistMeta`, `update_video_annotations`?).
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:33:Also re-verify: baseline-advance correctness on every branch; money-safety (no enqueue, no `spend_ledger`, no regenerable-cache resurrection, `needsRegen` report-only, and any path forcing the USER to re-spend counts); atomicity; idempotency reasoned across TWO runs; cross-backend mismatches; RLS/no-service-role.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:572:+- **Baseline advance.** Every branch either writes a baseline or throws before doing so:
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:588:+  baseline → re-evaluated. Verified per branch.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:639:+1. Does the guard cover EVERY consumer of a possibly-unreadable body, or only the two-sided reconcile path? Audit every other `readMdBody` / `blob.get` call in `lib/cloud-sync/*` — companion (`companion.ts`, `decideCompanion` / `sourceMdHash` comparison), `transferClassA`'s own reads, the manifest/backfill paths, `registry.ts`. Can a null-because-unreadable still be read as a semantic fact anywhere else (e.g. "the model was not generated from this MD" → deleting a companion model, or a Class-B/backfill decision)?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:640:+2. Is throwing the right response versus skipping? A record that PERMANENTLY advertises a `summaryMd` whose blob is genuinely gone (real storage drift, not transient) now errors on EVERY run forever and never advances a baseline. Trace what that does to delete-inference, to `report.errors` growth, and to a user's ability to ever complete a sync run. Is there a case where this new throw makes a previously-working sync fail?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:641:+3. Does the guard change behavior for the legitimate summary-less video (`summaryMd == null`) and the one-sided-hydration case (M-R2-2)? Both must still work. Confirm against the tests at `tests/integration/cloud-sync/e2e.int.test.ts`.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:642:+4. Are the two new B1 regression tests honest — do they fail for the RIGHT reason when the guard is removed, and do they assert across two runs?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:645:+The B1 root cause is a **semantic conflation across a module boundary**: a value that means "absent" in one module is produced by a failure in another. Hunt for siblings of that shape anywhere on the sync path — not just for blobs. Candidates worth tracing: a swallowed error that yields a default/empty value which a caller then treats as fact (empty index vs failed read; absent manifest vs unreadable manifest; a missing record vs a failed query; `readIndex` on a store error; `''` vs absent playlist metadata).
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:648:+- Baseline-advance correctness on every branch; no advance without a durable write; every "seen" video gets one (delete-inference).
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:650:+- Atomicity: durable-before-advertise, manifest-after-commit.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:2184:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-claude.md:94:### B1 (BLOCKING) — `mdHash == null` conflates "has no MD" with "MD is unreadable", so an unreadable blob silently destroys the other replica's body and launders it into an agreed baseline
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:2188:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-claude.md:106:deleted; seeds a cloud row advertising `summaryMd` + a `promoted` artifact with no readable body):
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:2206:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-claude.md:194:  new one-sided-hydration branches. The only laundering path found is B1.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:2217:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:29:1. Does the guard cover EVERY consumer of a possibly-unreadable body, or only the two-sided reconcile path? Audit every other `readMdBody` / `blob.get` call in `lib/cloud-sync/*` — companion (`companion.ts`, `decideCompanion` / `sourceMdHash` comparison), `transferClassA`'s own reads, the manifest/backfill paths, `registry.ts`. Can a null-because-unreadable still be read as a semantic fact anywhere else (e.g. "the model was not generated from this MD" → deleting a companion model, or a Class-B/backfill decision)?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:2218:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:30:2. Is throwing the right response versus skipping? A record that PERMANENTLY advertises a `summaryMd` whose blob is genuinely gone (real storage drift, not transient) now errors on EVERY run forever and never advances a baseline. Trace what that does to delete-inference, to `report.errors` growth, and to a user's ability to ever complete a sync run. Is there a case where this new throw makes a previously-working sync fail?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:2219:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:31:3. Does the guard change behavior for the legitimate summary-less video (`summaryMd == null`) and the one-sided-hydration case (M-R2-2)? Both must still work. Confirm against the tests at `tests/integration/cloud-sync/e2e.int.test.ts`.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:2220:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:32:4. Are the two new B1 regression tests honest — do they fail for the RIGHT reason when the guard is removed, and do they assert across two runs?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:2222:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v4-rereview-codex.md:35:The B1 root cause is a **semantic conflation across a module boundary**: a value that means "absent" in one module is produced by a failure in another. Hunt for siblings of that shape anywhere on the sync path — not just for blobs. Candidates worth tracing: a swallowed error that yields a default/empty value which a caller then treats as fact (empty index vs failed read; absent manifest vs unreadable manifest; a missing record vs a failed query; `readIndex` on a store error; `''` vs absent playlist metadata).

VERIFY: with `digDeeperMd` preserved but the MD BODY replaced by the winner's, is the retained dig doc now semantically stale in a way that misleads a consumer (`lib/html-doc/build-doc-html.ts:75,86`, `app/api/videos/[id]/dig-state/route.ts`, `lib/pdf/pdf-path.ts`)? Specifically: dig sections are anchored to summary section timestamps/anchors — if the winner MD has different sections, does merging the preserved dig produce wrong or orphaned anchors? Weigh that against the cost of destroying paid content. Is `digDeeperHtml: null` sufficient to force the re-merge? Is the additive path (`sanitizeAdditiveVideo`, which still nulls `digDeeperMd`) still correct given it targets a receiver with no existing row?
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:2454:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:25:3. **M-R2-2** — the corrections guard narrowed to `correctionsUnresolved && la.mdHash != null && ca.mdHash != null`, with `deriveClassASignals` hoisted above the guard. VERIFY: the hoist claims to be behavior-neutral because derivation is pure — confirm `readMdBody` has no side effects and that moving TWO blob reads earlier cannot change ordering/error behavior (e.g. a blob read that throws now aborts the video BEFORE the Class-B baseline would have been written — is that a behavior change, and is it the right one?). Confirm the WB-B1 intent still holds exactly for the both-have-MD case. Confirm the one-sided hydration case cannot destroy anything or record a false agreement.

VERIFY: is the guard placed BEFORE every write path (including the companion transfer and any archived/delete handling)? Does the `continue` skip anything that MUST still run (delete-inference "seen" marking, report counters, companion, archived sync)? Is `report.archivedNotSynced` incremented correctly and only there? Does the placeholder baseline (docVersionMajor 0) cause a wrong decision anywhere that DOES read the Class-A baseline — confirm reconcileClassA truly never reads it.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:2964:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:4774:  ● cloud-sync §10 end-to-end scenarios › WB-H1/H-R2-1: additive create with a promoted summaryMd but no blob throws before claiming a receiver slot; no row, no baseline (2 runs)
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:2978:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:4933:Evidence: [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:160) now throws before `ensureReceiverSlot` at line 164, so no `setPlaylistMeta`, staged blob write, `claimVideoSlot`, receiver row, or baseline can happen after an unreadable advertised MD body. The remaining `if (video.summaryMd && mdBody != null)` at line 167 is redundant after the guard, but harmless; it does not hide a path because the only alternative is a legitimate summary-less row. I temporarily moved the guard back and the H-R2-1 integration test failed on the bare local row assertion, then restored the file.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:2981:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:4939:Evidence: [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:491) derives signals before the narrowed guard, and [lib/cloud-sync/backfill.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/backfill.ts:7) is pure over the record plus supplied MD body. The hoist can surface blob-read errors earlier, but that is the right behavior: baseline must not advance if Class-A evidence is unreadable. The both-have-MD conflict path still takes the WB-B1 skip at [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:501). The one-sided hydration path writes the copied MD only after `transferClassA` lands, preserves unresolved corrections in Class-B baseline, and continues to report `needsRegen`.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:3394:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3114:lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:384: *  baseline) record an HONEST unresolved placeholder (mdHash null → "no agreed MD"); Class-A baseline
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:3395:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3121:WB-B1: GENUINELY FIXED. The guard is before Class-A signal derivation, transfer, companion transfer, normal archived counter, and normal baseline advance in [sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:471). It still logs Class-B conflicts before the guard, increments `needsRegen`, increments `archivedNotSynced` only for archived divergence, writes a baseline so delete inference has seen the video, then continues. `reconcileClassA` takes only live `ClassASignals`, not manifest Class-A baselines, so the `{ docVersionMajor: 0, mdHash: null }` placeholder is not read by the Class-A decision code.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:3398:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3132:Scenario: cloud-only video advertises `summaryMd`, but the MD blob is absent. Run 1 calls `ensureReceiverSlot` before the unreadable-body check, creating a bare local row, then throws at line 159 and does not write a baseline. Run 2 now sees both sides present, not an additive create. Since both MD body reads are null, `reconcileClassA` returns `skip`; `runSync` writes a manifest baseline at line 512 even though no receiver summary was ever durably copied and the source still advertises an unreadable summary. This masks the original corruption after one report and turns a failed additive copy into a false “seen/agreed no-MD” state.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:3399:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3134:Fix: validate `video.summaryMd`/`mdBody` before `ensureReceiverSlot`, or wrap `copyAdditiveVideo` in rollback that deletes the claimed receiver slot and any staged/final blob if any later step fails. The baseline should not be able to advance from a bare receiver slot created by a failed additive copy.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:3401:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3150:WB-B1: GENUINELY FIXED. The guard is before Class-A signal derivation, transfer, companion transfer, normal archived counter, and normal baseline advance in [sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:471). It still logs Class-B conflicts before the guard, increments `needsRegen`, increments `archivedNotSynced` only for archived divergence, writes a baseline so delete inference has seen the video, then continues. `reconcileClassA` takes only live `ClassASignals`, not manifest Class-A baselines, so the `{ docVersionMajor: 0, mdHash: null }` placeholder is not read by the Class-A decision code.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:3404:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3161:Scenario: cloud-only video advertises `summaryMd`, but the MD blob is absent. Run 1 calls `ensureReceiverSlot` before the unreadable-body check, creating a bare local row, then throws at line 159 and does not write a baseline. Run 2 now sees both sides present, not an additive create. Since both MD body reads are null, `reconcileClassA` returns `skip`; `runSync` writes a manifest baseline at line 512 even though no receiver summary was ever durably copied and the source still advertises an unreadable summary. This masks the original corruption after one report and turns a failed additive copy into a false “seen/agreed no-MD” state.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:3405:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:3163:Fix: validate `video.summaryMd`/`mdBody` before `ensureReceiverSlot`, or wrap `copyAdditiveVideo` in rollback that deletes the claimed receiver slot and any staged/final blob if any later step fails. The baseline should not be able to advance from a bare receiver slot created by a failed additive copy.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:3408:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-codex.md:28:3. **ATOMICITY.** Manifest baseline written ONLY after the receiver tuple verifies durable. Blob durable BEFORE the record advertises `artifacts.summaryMd.status='promoted'`. Additive create verifies the receiver row exists (readIndex) before the baseline (cloud upsertVideo silently no-ops on an absent row → ensureReceiverSlot creates it). transferClassA's fix (put-overwrite the final key with verified staged bytes, then updateVideoFields) preserves durable-before-finalize. Crash before verify leaves the baseline unadvanced (re-run heals).

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:4246:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-codex.md:7474:   Scenario: source video has `summaryMd` and `artifacts.summaryMd.status='promoted'`, but the source blob read returns `null` due to storage drift/corruption/RLS miss. `copyAdditiveVideo` skips the blob write because `mdBody == null`, but `sanitizeAdditiveVideo` preserves the sender’s `artifacts.summaryMd`. The post-write verify must check the advertised artifact tuple, not just row presence.

The guard is before Class-A signal derivation, transfer, companion transfer, normal archived counter, and normal baseline advance in [sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:471). It still logs Class-B conflicts before the guard, increments `needsRegen`, increments `archivedNotSynced` only for archived divergence, writes a baseline so delete inference has seen the video, then continues. `reconcileClassA` takes only live `ClassASignals`, not manifest Class-A baselines, so the `{ docVersionMajor: 0, mdHash: null }` placeholder is not read by the Class-A decision code.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:8183:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:4933:Evidence: [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:160) now throws before `ensureReceiverSlot` at line 164, so no `setPlaylistMeta`, staged blob write, `claimVideoSlot`, receiver row, or baseline can happen after an unreadable advertised MD body. The remaining `if (video.summaryMd && mdBody != null)` at line 167 is redundant after the guard, but harmless; it does not hide a path because the only alternative is a legitimate summary-less row. I temporarily moved the guard back and the H-R2-1 integration test failed on the bare local row assertion, then restored the file.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:8186:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:4939:Evidence: [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:491) derives signals before the narrowed guard, and [lib/cloud-sync/backfill.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/backfill.ts:7) is pure over the record plus supplied MD body. The hoist can surface blob-read errors earlier, but that is the right behavior: baseline must not advance if Class-A evidence is unreadable. The both-have-MD conflict path still takes the WB-B1 skip at [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:501). The one-sided hydration path writes the copied MD only after `transferClassA` lands, preserves unresolved corrections in Class-B baseline, and continues to report `needsRegen`.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:8191:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:4959:Evidence: [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:160) now throws before `ensureReceiverSlot` at line 164, so no `setPlaylistMeta`, staged blob write, `claimVideoSlot`, receiver row, or baseline can happen after an unreadable advertised MD body. The remaining `if (video.summaryMd && mdBody != null)` at line 167 is redundant after the guard, but harmless; it does not hide a path because the only alternative is a legitimate summary-less row. I temporarily moved the guard back and the H-R2-1 integration test failed on the bare local row assertion, then restored the file.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:8194:+docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v3-rereview-codex.md:4965:Evidence: [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:491) derives signals before the narrowed guard, and [lib/cloud-sync/backfill.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/backfill.ts:7) is pure over the record plus supplied MD body. The hoist can surface blob-read errors earlier, but that is the right behavior: baseline must not advance if Class-A evidence is unreadable. The both-have-MD conflict path still takes the WB-B1 skip at [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:501). The one-sided hydration path writes the copied MD only after `transferClassA` lands, preserves unresolved corrections in Class-B baseline, and continues to report `needsRegen`.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:8523:+- The one-sided/additive path was already guarded at [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:160), before receiver slot creation.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:8527:+- M-R2-2 still works: a real one-sided MD hydration with the other side `summaryMd == null` is not blocked.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:8529:+Throwing is the right response for this fix. A permanently missing advertised blob will produce a per-video `report.errors` entry every run, but `runSync` catches per video and continues processing the rest. More importantly, it does not write a false baseline, does not infer agreement, and does not overwrite the other side. The user can repair by restoring the blob, clearing the stale `summaryMd`, or regenerating intentionally.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:8531:+The two new B1 regression tests are honest. They seed a row that advertises promoted `summaryMd` while omitting the blob, assert byte preservation, assert no baseline, and repeat across two runs. Removing the guard would fail on the intended destructive-copy/laundering assertions.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:8550:+  - baseline advance
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:8551:+- The one-sided/additive path was already guarded at [lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts](/Users/kujinlee/code/agentic-ai-docs/youtube-playlist-summaries-cloud/lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts:160), before receiver slot creation.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:8555:+- M-R2-2 still works: a real one-sided MD hydration with the other side `summaryMd == null` is not blocked.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:8557:+Throwing is the right response for this fix. A permanently missing advertised blob will produce a per-video `report.errors` entry every run, but `runSync` catches per video and continues processing the rest. More importantly, it does not write a false baseline, does not infer agreement, and does not overwrite the other side. The user can repair by restoring the blob, clearing the stale `summaryMd`, or regenerating intentionally.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:8559:+The two new B1 regression tests are honest. They seed a row that advertises promoted `summaryMd` while omitting the blob, assert byte preservation, assert no baseline, and repeat across two runs. Removing the guard would fail on the intended destructive-copy/laundering assertions.

docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v5-rereview-codex.md:12352:A concrete issue has emerged in `readManifest`: it still collapses missing, corrupt, and unreadable into the empty baseline. That is not just telemetry loss; for one-sided videos it changes delete inference into additive resurrection. I’m validating this against the spec/review trail before deciding severity.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:19:1. **WB-B1 (Blocking) — corrections no-write conflict drove a destructive Class-A copy.** Fix: in `runSync` (`lib/cloud-sync/sync-run.ts`), when `merges.corrections.winner === 'equal' && merges.corrections.conflict`, skip the Class-A copy entirely, count `needsRegen`, and write `buildCorrectionsUnresolvedBaseline` (carries the PREVIOUS classA baseline, or an honest `{docVersionMajor:0, mdGeneratedAt:null, mdCorrectionsHash:null, mdHash:null}` placeholder on first sync). VERIFY: is the guard placed BEFORE every write path (including the companion transfer and any archived/delete handling)? Does the `continue` skip anything that MUST still run (delete-inference "seen" marking, report counters, companion, archived sync)? Is `report.archivedNotSynced` incremented correctly and only there? Does the placeholder baseline (docVersionMajor 0) cause a wrong decision anywhere that DOES read the Class-A baseline — confirm reconcileClassA truly never reads it.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:25:- Baseline-advance correctness across ALL branches now (advance / carry-previous / placeholder / skip) — any branch that advances a baseline without a corresponding durable write, or fails to advance for a "seen" video (breaking delete-inference → a spurious delete next run).
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:27:- Atomicity: durable-before-advertise, manifest-after-commit still holds on every path.
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:58://    so persist_summary is unreachable) and advance the manifest baseline ONLY after the receiver
docs/reviews/whole-branch-cloud-sync-v2-rereview-codex.md:59://    tuple is verified durable — stage → verify → promote → finalize → verify → baseline (F2).
